read_more_only_when: startup context is stale or ambiguous
gameplay evidence contradicts the checkpoint handoff
action_verification_policy: Do not trust click success by itself for gameplay actions.
Capture the minimum relevant before/after state for the action you are taking.
Treat missing intended state mutation as an action failure and use the recipe fallback before continuing.

authoritative_state.next_action: Resume Persona A from the authoritative Week 8 checkpoint or live summary state in `phase11-safe-grinder`, advance exactly one completed in-game week, start from the Week 8 summary anchor `₡458 / Debt ₡0 / Hunger 60% / Sanity 25 / Trauma Reboot 266h`, and verify whether the restored `Sanitation-T3` plus `Work Shift x3` route still survives from the harsher hunger floor. Treat GitHub issues #7, #8, and #9 as fixed baseline behavior unless a fresh Week 9 run again shows a one-choice week opener after requirement filtering, `workflow:phase11:checkpoint:status` / `workflow:phase11:checkpoint:export` stop reporting `browser_state_source: live_session` while the live browser page is already authoritative, or visible onboarding is still misclassified as `live_continuity` instead of forcing checkpoint recovery.
active_findings: `TRAUMA_REBOOT` does not remove the deterministic low-risk labor route by itself. Safe Grinder still recovers the full `Work Shift x3` line immediately in Week 8 once the week opens without the old f…
The live build did not reproduce GitHub issue `#7` during this slice. Advancing from the Week 7 summary led to direct Persona A control instead of another one-choice global opener.
The Week 8 closing summary reconciles cleanly again. The visible sanity rows total `-10`, `pendingTurnSummary.totals.sanityChange` also reports `-10`, and the live end state lands at `25` sanity.
followups: **Checkpoint Recovery:** Startup continuity verification was a false positive for this slice. Preserve the Week 7 checkpoint as the recovery anchor until the runner/browser continuity layer stops reo…

**Checkpoint Export:** Authoritative Week 8 state exported to `docs/workflows/cyberpunk-overhaul/checkpoints/persona_a/week-08-save.json` with metadata at `docs/workflows/cyberpunk-overhaul/checkpoin…

trusted_ui_workarounds: Use stable action-card selectors first for jobs and shopping; verify state changed before assuming success.
If onboarding or a reset appears unexpectedly, capture a screenshot and run a short body-text probe before clicking through.
If live continuity is missing but a checkpoint file exists, restore it before replaying from onboarding.
For travel, apply, shopping, and work-shift clicks, compare the relevant before/after state instead of trusting the click response alone.

global_rules: Prefer targeted eval probes that return compact JSON over document-wide text dumps.
Prefer stable data-testid selectors first; verify the resulting game state after any job application or purchase.
Do not trust a successful click response by itself; confirm the intended before/after gameplay mutation for travel, apply, shopping, and work-shift actions.

session_recovery.goal: Recover authoritative play when the named browser session opens on onboarding or has no save state.

actions: Click the named city button by its `data-testid` first; city travel cards are now semantic `<button>` controls rather than custom clickable divs.
Use the location-specific `city-travel-card-*` selector instead of broad text scans or full DOM dumps whenever the destination is known.
Only fall back to targeted DOM evaluation if the expected `city-travel-card-*` control is absent or the post-click state verification fails.
verify: Check the active location label or dashboard title.
Check player time after travel when the destination should cost time.
Treat a successful click response without location or time change as a failed travel attempt.

apply_job.goal: Secure a labor shift job such as Sanitation-T3.
actions: Call `agent-browser scrollintoview` on the targeted job button before any direct click so the pointer action lands on an in-viewport control.
Click the button selector first once it is in view; only fall back to the card selector if the button is absent.
Immediately verify CURRENT SHIFT or persisted `careerLevel` before continuing the slice.
verify: Check CURRENT SHIFT or the persisted game state for the selected job id.
Treat a successful click response without state mutation as a failed apply attempt.
fallbacks: Re-query the specific job card and button only, not the whole DOM.
If the first direct click reports success without state change, re-run `scrollintoview`, click once more, and verify `careerLevel` or CURRENT SHIFT again before escalating to targeted DOM evaluation.

verify: Confirm credits changed by the expected amount.
Confirm hunger or sanity moved in the expected direction.
Treat a successful click response without the expected stat deltas as a failed purchase attempt.

work_shift.goal: Execute a labor shift once the current job is active.
actions: Find the visible WORK SHIFT control only after the Labor Sector dashboard is confirmed active.
After each shift, re-check credits, time, hunger, sanity, and whether a turn summary or event modal interrupted the route.
verify: Credits increase by the shift yield.
Time drops by the expected shift cost.
Any forced event or summary modal is detected immediately.

turn_summary_probe.goal: Capture the authoritative end-of-week state with minimal context cost.
